mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate has plateaued at 5.25% after a prolonged tightening cycle ending mid-2006. A stable-to-flat FEDFUNDS reading following a hiking cycle signals the Fed believes inflation risk is contained, reducing discount-rate uncertainty and supporting equity valuations over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% since June 2006 after 17 consecutive hikes
regime_hint: monetary policy pause after tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS plateau duration and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX posts negative 20d returns in more than 45% of plateau months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fell sharply from prior highs (revised value 1650k vs earlier peaks above 2000k). A decline in HOUST exceeding 15% year-over-year signals a housing-led demand contraction that transmits to broader consumer spending and corporate earnings within one quarter, depressing S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1665k Aug2006 down from 2005 highs near 2100k
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a >15% YoY HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of triggered historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to expand at a healthy pace while the Fed funds rate remains elevated at cycle highs, this combination signals a 'soft landing' where labor strength offsets tightening risk, supporting equity risk appetite over the next month. The mechanism requires joint confirmation of both series since either alone is ambiguous.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 136826k Sep2006 continuing gains despite FEDFUNDS at 5.25%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive but stable policy
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (<0.02) versus PAYEMS-only model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating while the Fed maintains a restrictive federal funds rate indicates supply-side momentum is fading under tight monetary conditions, a pattern historically preceding equity market weakness during late-cycle tightening regimes. Outside tightening regimes the same INDPRO deceleration does not reliably predict returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at cycle peak 5.25% while INDPRO growth moderating in 2006
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 5%, INDPRO deceleration is not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns in a majority of historical windows, invalidating the regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE at 4.5-4.6%) reflects tight labor markets that sustain household income and consumption, providing a persistent tailwind to equity prices over short horizons as consumer-driven earnings expectations remain firm.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised_value 4.5% Sep2006, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between low UNRATE level and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 across the full 2005-2024 sample, or returns show no statistically significant difference from unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth slowing) below recent trend signals reduced inflationary pressure, raising market expectations of future Fed rate cuts and lowering the equity risk premium within the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized growth falls below 2.5% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value 203.8 Aug2006, moderating from summer 2006 highs
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI deceleration triggers are negative in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value 351240M Sep2006 showing continued consumer spending growth
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly RSAFS growth sign and subsequent 5d SPX return direction is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive but industrial production (INDPRO) simultaneously contracts, this divergence signals an emerging stagflation-like supply imbalance that is particularly damaging to equities when the economy is in a late-cycle high-rate regime, as margin compression risk rises without offsetting rate relief.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth positive while INDPRO 3-month change turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value 11388.1 Q1-2006 still growing while INDPRO growth softening into Q3 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle high interest rate regime with supply-demand divergence
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS >= 5% periods, GDP-INDPRO divergence episodes fail to produce below-median 60d SPX returns in a majority of historical cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
